name: Admin routes and session lifecycle
description:
## Goal
Build admin workflow for creating sessions, sharing links, and monitoring progress in realtime.

## Status
On hold pending schema consensus task tilkkziu. No implementation should proceed until consensus is approved.

## Scope
- Add routes: /admin, /admin/sessions/new, /admin/sessions/[sessionId].
- Create session action from workspace.
- Show session status, participant identity, and round progress.
- Provide participant link copy/share affordance.

## Files
- src/routes/admin/+page.svelte
- src/routes/admin/sessions/new/+page.svelte
- src/routes/admin/sessions/[sessionId]/+page.svelte
- src/lib/components/date-picker-study/admin/*

## Acceptance Criteria
- Admin can create sessions repeatedly.
- Session list updates from Jazz data.
- Admin can open a session detail page and copy participant URL.
result: Admin routes and session lifecycle implemented end-to-end. Account bootstrap populates study_session_index on creation via Jazz withMigration. createAdminSession creates a per-session public-writable Group so the session URL alone is the participant access credential; the factory threads ownership through every nested CoValue. Dashboard lists sessions with status + round/challenge progress, /admin/sessions/new offers a challenges_per_picker override and redirects to the detail route, /admin/sessions/[sessionId] exposes a copy-participant-URL button and per-round progress. Static challenge catalog and canonical picker list added; factory validates shape at creation time. Verification: pnpm --ignore-workspace check (0 errors, 0 warnings), prettier + eslint clean on touched files. Browser sanity check: /admin loads behind passkey auth; end-to-end UI loop not exercised because Chrome DevTools MCP cannot complete passkey prompts. pnpm build fails on the repo's uncommitted Sentry instrumentation + adapter-auto combo (pre-existing, out of scope).

commit.message: Add admin routes, session lifecycle, and per-session public groups

name: Participant route state machine
description:
## Goal
Implement /session/[sessionId] as a single route that renders identity, challenge, rating, and completion states directly from Jazz session state.

## Scope
- Add route /session/[sessionId].
- Bind participant_id and first_name on start.
- Derive visible screen from session.status, current_round_index, and round status.
- Ensure reload/resume lands on exact in-progress step.

## Files
- src/routes/session/[sessionId]/+page.svelte
- src/lib/components/date-picker-study/participant/*

## Acceptance Criteria
- Participant can enter identity and start session.
- UI rehydrates into the correct step after refresh.
- No parallel local workflow state diverges from Jazz.
result: Participant route state machine wired. /session/[sessionId] loads the session with STUDY_SESSION_RESOLVE, handles loading/unavailable/unauthorized, then selects the view by session.status and (for in_progress) round.status. IdentityForm + startParticipantSession handle the pending_participant → in_progress transition (identity bind, timezone/locale, started_at_ms, status flip, throws on empty identity or wrong status). RoundView renders current challenge prompt + compact progress from Jazz with no mirror state; RatingView renders the rating heading (star inputs + submission belong to jd1z5xv7). CompletionScreen renders the thanks view. Reload/resume lands on the exact in-progress step because every branch is a pure function of session state. Verification: pnpm check (0 errors / 0 warnings), prettier + eslint clean on touched files. Not exercised in browser for this task.

name: Freeze study configuration
description:
## Goal
Lock open study decisions into explicit defaults before implementation.

## Decisions
- Set challenges_per_picker default to 5.
- Allow per-session challenges_per_picker override at session creation.
- Remove export from scope.
- Exclude free-text comments entirely.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Decisions are recorded in Dex and reflected in downstream task scope.
result: Decisions finalized: challenges_per_picker defaults to 5 with a per-session override at create time; export is not in v1 scope; free-text comments are excluded from v1.

name: Date picker usability study
description:
## Goal
Build the study app described in DATE_PICKER_USABILITY_STUDY_SPEC.md using Jazz + SvelteKit + Graffiti.

## Why
The repo is currently a minimal SvelteKit scaffold, so we need a structured implementation plan that delivers participant flow, admin flow, persistence, and metrics without speculative refactors.

## Working Agreement
- Use Jazz MCP docs and project-relevant skills for implementation decisions.
- Use svelte-file-editor subagent for .svelte creation/edits and run Svelte autofixer before handoff.
- Apply html and ux-interface-design guidance to participant/admin UI structure and clarity.
- Do not create git commits until explicit user code approval.

## Done When
- Core study flows are implemented end-to-end.
- Data model and state transitions are persisted in Jazz.
- Instrumentation and validation steps are documented in task results.

name: Core app wiring and auth shell
description:
## Goal
Set up baseline app wiring for Jazz provider, account auth UI, and route shell before study features land.

## Scope
- Wrap app with JazzSvelteProvider in root layout.
- Add PasskeyAuthBasicUI gating for signed-in flows.
- Ensure sync config and provider lifecycle are centralized in layout.
- Establish shared app shell navigation to /admin and participant routes.

## Files
- src/routes/+layout.svelte
- src/routes/+page.svelte

## Acceptance Criteria
- App boots with Jazz provider and auth UI.
- Signed-in user can navigate to /admin and /session/[sessionId].
- No duplicated provider setup in child routes.
result: Core wiring finalized with your layout direction: root layout remains minimal with JazzSvelteProvider only, admin auth is isolated to src/routes/admin/+layout.svelte via PasskeyAuthBasicUI, and default route stays intentionally minimal for manual UX work.

name: Rating capture and completion
description:
## Goal
Capture post-round ratings with auto-submit and finalize sessions correctly.

## Scope
- Build 3-dimension star rating component (design, ease_of_use, magicalness).
- Auto-submit rating when all dimensions have values.
- Complete session only when all rounds and ratings are done.

## Files
- src/lib/components/date-picker-study/rating/*
- src/routes/admin/sessions/[sessionId]/+page.svelte

## Acceptance Criteria
- Ratings persist for every round.
- Session status switches to completed only at full completion.
result: Rating capture and session finalization complete. StarRating is a reusable 5-radio control using the provided star SVG with currentColor fill; visually-hidden inputs keep keyboard and screen-reader accessibility intact via fieldset/legend, filled state toggles opacity 0.4 → 1. RatingForm shows all three dimensions at once (design, ease of use, magicalness), persists each star pick to Jazz immediately so partial state survives reload, and gates round advancement behind an explicit Submit button that stays disabled until every dimension is set. Submit calls completeRoundAfterRating which advances current_round_index or flips session.status to completed when all rounds are done. Participant state machine renders RatingForm directly (the thin RatingView wrapper was removed). Admin session detail page now outputs full session data: metadata, participant link, per-round ratings + timings, per-challenge table with prompt/target/final/correctness/time/attempts/clicks/keypresses. Dropped rating_submitted_at_ms field from the schema per product decision — not tracked. Verification: pnpm check (0 errors / 0 warnings), prettier + eslint clean on touched files, /session and /admin/sessions routes compile and serve. End-to-end participant-through-rating walkthrough still needs a real session (passkey auth blocker for MCP), but every component and state transition is in place.

commit.message: Add rating capture and full admin session detail

name: Challenge engine and round progression
description:
## Goal
Build shared challenge execution logic that measures correctness and interaction metrics independent of picker implementation.

## Scope
- Create normalized date comparison helpers (YYYY-MM-DD).
- Start timing when challenge becomes interactive and stop on first correct value.
- Track attempt_count, click_count, keypress_count by wrapper scope.
- Auto-complete challenge on strict equality and advance round.

## Files
- src/lib/utils/date-normalization.ts
- src/lib/components/date-picker-study/engine/*
- src/lib/components/date-picker-study/challenge-runner/*

## Acceptance Criteria
- Each challenge run persists elapsed_ms, correctness, attempts, clicks, keypresses.
- Auto-advance works after short confirmation delay.
- Round status transitions pending -> in_progress -> awaiting_rating -> completed.
result: Challenge engine + round/session progression in place. date-normalization utility (isPlainDateKey, normalizePlainDateKey, plainDateKeysEqual) handles strict YYYY-MM-DD comparison. picker-protocol defines the study-picker-change CustomEvent contract (composed+bubbles) that re77y6p1 adapters will dispatch. progression helpers own every lifecycle transition in one module: startRoundIfPending flips pending→in_progress, markRunShown stamps timing + zeroes counters only on first view, completeRun records correctness/final value/elapsed_ms, advanceAfterCorrectChallenge advances to next run or closes round to awaiting_rating with total/average elapsed, and completeRoundAfterRating is the entry point jd1z5xv7 calls after rating submit (advances round or flips session to completed). ChallengeRunner hosts the wrapper, attaches wrapper-scoped click/keydown/study-picker-change listeners, increments attempt_count for distinct non-matching values, skips pure modifier keys per spec §8.5, and auto-advances 800ms after the matching emission with a 'Got it' ack during the dwell. Participant state machine now routes to ChallengeRunner; the old RoundView placeholder is gone. Verification: pnpm check (0 errors / 0 warnings), prettier + eslint clean. Not exercised in browser — no picker adapter exists yet to fire study-picker-change events; end-to-end verification unlocks when re77y6p1 lands.

commit.message: Add challenge engine and round progression

name: Sentry metrics instrumentation and validation
description:
## Goal
Implement the study metric catalog and verify emitted data in Sentry during end-to-end runs.

## Scope
- Add @sentry/sveltekit setup with metrics support.
- Emit challenge and input_round metrics with standard attributes.
- Enforce privacy/cardinality rules (no prompt text, no first_name).
- Validate metric arrival and dimensional breakdown in Sentry UI.

## Files
- src/hooks.client.ts
- src/hooks.server.ts
- src/lib/components/date-picker-study/metrics/*

## Acceptance Criteria
- All metrics in spec section 8 emit at correct workflow transitions.
- Attributes include session_id, participant_id, picker_id, challenge_id, indexes, timezone, locale, device_class.
- Validation evidence recorded in task result.
result: Sentry metrics instrumentation complete. metrics/emit.ts wraps every metric in spec §8.2 behind typed emitters — emitChallengeResolved fires study.challenge.completed + duration_ms + attempt_count + click_count + keypress_count as one batch, emitRoundCompleted fires study.input_round.completed + total_duration_ms and three study.input_round.rating distributions keyed by dimension. Standard attribute set (session_id, picker_id, input_round_index, challenge_id, challenge_index, participant_id, timezone, locale, device_class) is built once and shared; privacy/cardinality respected per §8.4 — participant_first_name and prompt_text are never read. ChallengeRunner emits on correct completion before the 800ms confirmation dwell, pinning the round index so the metric reflects the round the challenge belonged to. RatingForm pins the round ref + index before completeRoundAfterRating (which may advance current_round_index), then emits the round metrics and calls flushStudyMetrics() when the session flips to 'completed' per §8.1. Uses Sentry.metrics.count and Sentry.metrics.distribution from @sentry/sveltekit 10.49.0 with 'millisecond' units on duration metrics. Verification: pnpm check (0 errors), prettier + eslint clean. Sentry UI validation is NOT done in this task — admin has to create a session, participant runs through, then check the 8 metric names appear in Sentry with the expected attribute breakdowns. Integration walkthrough is blocked by passkey auth for DevTools MCP; user will validate.

commit.message: Add Sentry metrics for challenge and round outcomes

name: Picker adapter interface and integrations
description:
## Goal
Integrate all three date pickers through one adapter contract that emits normalized plain-date values.

## Scope
- Define shared adapter interface for value read/write, events, and lifecycle.
- Build adapter wrappers for picker A, picker B, picker C web components.
- Ensure emitted values normalize to YYYY-MM-DD for correctness checks.
- Keep correctness engine picker-agnostic.

## Files
- src/lib/components/date-picker-study/pickers/adapter.ts
- src/lib/components/date-picker-study/pickers/*

## Acceptance Criteria
- Participant can complete rounds across all 3 pickers.
- Picker order assignment follows configured counterbalancing strategy.
- No picker-specific logic leaks into challenge evaluator.
result: Picker adapter contract in place and three placeholder web components registered. Contract documented in engine/picker-protocol.ts as a StudyPickerElement interface: each picker exposes a value:string property (YYYY-MM-DD, loose form tolerated) and fires the standard input/change DOM events when the value changes — no custom event name, no detail payload, so any installed form-like web component plugs in with minimal or zero adapter code. pickers/adapter.ts derives the custom-element tag from picker_id (input_a → study-input-a) and is the one place to replace the mapping when real pickers arrive. pickers/placeholder-date-picker.ts wraps <input type='date'> with a value getter/setter and lets native events bubble unchanged. pickers/register.ts registers one subclass per picker_id inside a browser guard + dynamic imports so the DOM-dependent class stays out of the SSR bundle. ChallengeRunner listens for input and change on the wrapper and reads .value off the picker element; {#key run.$jazz.id} replaces the picker between challenges for a clean state. Relocated pickers.ts to pickers/catalog.ts to keep the pickers/ directory unambiguous; session-factory and admin-workspace import from the new path. Verified in browser: all three tags register, picker.value proxies correctly, input and change bubble to the wrapper with picker.value current each time. Verification: pnpm check (0 errors), prettier + eslint clean. End-to-end participant flow through all three rounds still requires admin auth to create a session (Chrome DevTools MCP can't complete passkey), so the integration test is pending real-session testing.

commit.message: Add picker adapter contract + placeholder web components

name: Schema consensus and rewrite (no organizations)
description:
## Goal
Rework study schema design to remove organization concepts and align with agreed product model: admin accounts only, participant access without account signup.

## Locked Decisions
1) Participant access: session ID alone in URL is sufficient (no token/secret).
2) Round/challenge assignment timing: materialize at admin session creation time.
3) Admin edit scope: sessions are shared admin resources; any authenticated admin can edit any session.

## Scope
- Rewrite DATE_PICKER_USABILITY_STUDY_SPEC.md ownership/access sections to remove organization assumptions.
- Define final ownership and access model in plain terms.
- After approval, implement schema changes in src/lib/schema.ts and study schema modules.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Written consensus captured in spec and task result before any schema implementation resumes.
- Existing schema code changes are rolled back before re-implementation.
result: Consensus is captured in DATE_PICKER_USABILITY_STUDY_SPEC.md (no organizations, session-id-only participant access, assignment at session creation). Reimplemented schema from the approved contract: added StudyChallengeRun/StudyInputRound/StudySession/StudySessionIndex plus resolve queries in src/lib/components/date-picker-study/schema.ts, added session materialization helpers (balanced picker-order utility + createStudySession) in src/lib/components/date-picker-study/session-factory.ts, and defined the account root linkage study_session_index in src/lib/schema.ts via RateDateAccount. Verification: pnpm --ignore-workspace check (pass), pnpm --ignore-workspace build (pass), pnpm --ignore-workspace exec eslint on modified files (pass).

name: Study schema and org workspace wiring
description:
## Goal
Implement the Jazz study data model and connect one StudyWorkspace to each organization.

## Status
Superseded by task tilkkziu after product direction changed: no organization concept and no participant account requirement. Treat this task as historical and do not continue implementation from it.

## Scope
- Add StudyWorkspace, StudySession, StudyInputRound, StudyChallengeRun schemas.
- Add optional organization.study_workspace reference.
- Create deterministic workspace resolution and session creation helpers.
- Keep all schema fields additive and migration-light.

## Files
- src/lib/schema.ts
- src/lib/components/date-picker-study/schema.ts
- src/lib/components/date-picker-study/workspace.ts

## Acceptance Criteria
- Admin can create a StudySession under an org workspace.
- Session can be loaded by CoValue id for participant route.
- Types compile with strict TypeScript and no any usage.
result: Implemented study schema foundation and org wiring. Added StudyChallengeRun/StudyInputRound/StudySession/StudyWorkspace schemas, account schema with organizations and optional study_workspace ref, workspace/session helper module for creating/loading org workspaces and creating pending sessions, and wired JazzSvelteProvider to use RateDateAccount. Verification: pnpm --ignore-workspace check (pass), pnpm --ignore-workspace build (pass).

name: Pilot readiness and verification
description:
## Goal
Run final quality checks and prepare the app for an internal pilot.

## Scope
- Verify reload/resume for in-progress sessions.
- Verify assignment symmetry and counterbalanced picker order distribution.
- Run lint, check, and build and fix failures.
- Record known limitations and next-step tuning notes.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Local verification passes: pnpm lint, pnpm check, pnpm build.
- At least one full admin-to-participant run completes successfully.
- Pilot checklist and follow-up tuning notes captured in task result.
